title: Which Al Pacino Character Are You, Deep Down?
dek: Nine questions about ambition, temper, loyalty, and morality stand between you and your true Pacino alter ego — choose carefully, this is basically a legal deposition.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just answer like nobody's watching, then accept your fate.

questions.question: Your boss just handed you way more responsibility than you asked for. What's your gut reaction?

answers.text: I didn't want this life, but fine — I'll run it my way now.
Say hello to my new title. I earned this.
I'll do it right, even if it makes me everyone's least favorite coworker.
Great, more people to keep an eye on.

questions.question: Someone close to you crosses a line. How do you handle it?

answers.text: Loudly. Immediately. With a lot of yelling and a very large gun nearby.
I report it. Even if it costs me every friend I have.
I get obsessive about proving it, then I confront them head-on.
I let them have it with both barrels — verbally, at least.
Alone, doing the right thing nobody will thank me for.
Staking out a lead until 4 a.m. because I can't sleep anyway.
A five-star hotel, a bottle of something strong, and a tango with a stranger.
Trying to go straight, but somehow ending up back at the club.
I bend them constantly in pursuit of the bigger truth.
I only follow the ones about honor. Everything else is optional.
I already broke every rule once. Now I'm just trying not to again.
Rules are negotiable if you're persuasive enough.
Loyalty is standing up for someone even when it's not your fight.
Loyalty to the old life is exactly what keeps dragging me back in.
Loyalty is whoever's buying this quarter.
Loyalty is a contract. I always read the fine print.

questions.question: You just got some brutal, humiliating news in front of a room full of people. Reaction?

answers.text: I keep my cool, because losing it never actually helps me.
I turn it into a pitch. Somehow.
I smirk, because I already knew this was coming.
Total stillness. I'll deal with you later, in private, permanently.
I'll say literally anything to close a deal.
I genuinely enjoy watching people ruin themselves.
I isolate the people who actually love me.
I trust absolutely no one, including myself sometimes.

questions.question: Someone offers you a shortcut to get everything you want, no consequences (allegedly). Do you take it?

answers.text: I AM the shortcut. I'm the one offering it to you.
I take it, then spend the rest of my life paying for it anyway.
Obviously. Consequences are for people who think small.
No. The whole point is there ARE consequences, and I still tell the truth.

questions.question: How do people who know you best would describe you in one phrase?

answers.text: "Calm right up until the moment he isn't."
"Say hello to my little friend energy, 24/7."
"The one honest guy in a very dishonest room."
"Married to the job. Literally can't stop working."

questions.question: Last one: what's actually driving you underneath it all?

answers.text: Proving I came from nothing and now I own everything.
Being able to look at myself in the mirror.
The obsessive need to be the best at what I do.
Finding one reason left to actually want to live.

results.name: Michael Corleone
results.subtitle: The Godfather (1972)
results.description: You're calm, calculating, and terrifyingly patient — the kind of person who says "it's not personal" right before it gets extremely personal. You'd burn your whole life down to protect your family, and honestly, you might already have.

results.name: Tony Montana
results.subtitle: Scarface (1983)
results.description: You want it all, and you want people to watch you get it. Subtlety was never your strong suit — you'd rather announce your ambition at full volume than let anyone doubt it for a second.

results.name: Frank Serpico
results.subtitle: Serpico (1973)
results.description: You'd rather be right and alone than corrupt and surrounded. Everyone around you may be cutting corners, but you're the one lunatic still doing the actual paperwork.

results.name: Vincent Hanna
results.subtitle: Heat (1995)
results.description: You're obsessive, restless, and married to your work in every sense that matters. You'd have coffee with your own worst enemy just to talk shop — and you kind of already have.

results.name: Lt. Col. Frank Slade
results.subtitle: Scent of a Woman (1992)
results.description: Loud, blunt, and secretly the most decent person in the room once you push past the bluster. You'd deliver a monologue nobody asked for and somehow win everyone over anyway.

results.name: Carlito Brigante
results.subtitle: Carlito's Way (1993)
results.description: You're trying so hard to leave your old life behind, but it keeps finding you anyway. You've got real code, real regret, and a nagging feeling the finish line keeps moving.

results.name: Ricky Roma
results.subtitle: Glengarry Glen Ross (1992)
results.description: You could sell literally anything to literally anyone, and you take a strange pride in it. Ambition isn't a personality trait for you — it's basically the whole job description.

results.name: John Milton
results.subtitle: The Devil's Advocate (1997)
results.description: You're charming, patient, and always three steps ahead of everyone else's worst impulse. You don't have to twist anyone's arm — you just wait for people to talk themselves into it.
